mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration triggers risk-off positioning and lower earnings expectations, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts signal a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, rising short-term rates combined with contracting real GDP growth signal profit margin compression, leading to negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when both FEDFUNDS and GDPC1 exhibit the specified adverse pattern.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding corporate revenue bases, supporting higher equity valuations and positive forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth against 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 3.5 percent year-over-year, inflation expectations anchor higher, forcing equity risk premiums upward and depressing forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower when CPIAUCSL crosses the 3.5 percent threshold compared to periods below it.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong payroll growth and falling industrial production indicates a services-led economy with weakening manufacturing demand, creating earnings uncertainty that reduces forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to produce negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid credit expansion, surging residential construction combined with stable short-term rates amplifies household wealth effects, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant premium during housing booms when short-term rates remain stable.
